title: Endpoint Compliance Scoring
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:posture" earliest=-4h
| eval check_pass=if(match(lower(posture_status),"(?i)compliant"),1,0)
| stats avg(check_pass) as score by endpoint_mac
| eval compliance_pct=round(score*100,1)
| where compliance_pct < 80
| sort compliance_pct
| head 100
description: Composite score per endpoint from posture checks (AV, patch, encryption) for executive dashboards and exception reporting.
value: Composite score per endpoint from posture checks (AV, patch, encryption) for executive dashboards and exception reporting.
implementation: For multi-check rows per MAC, use `latest` per check name then average. Weight critical checks in `eval` if needed.
visualization: Table (lowest-scoring endpoints), Histogram (score distribution), Gauge (fleet average score).
knownFalsePositives: Posture failures during scheduled patch cycles, OS upgrades, or after AV definition rotations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk_TA_cisco-ise.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ise:posture`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
For multi-check rows per MAC, use `latest` per check name then average. Weight critical checks in `eval` if needed.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:posture" earliest=-4h
| eval check_pass=if(match(lower(posture_status),"(?i)compliant"),1,0)
| stats avg(check_pass) as score by endpoint_mac
| eval compliance_pct=round(score*100,1)
| where compliance_pct < 80
| sort compliance_pct
| head 100
```

Understanding this SPL

**Endpoint Compliance Scoring** — Composite score per endpoint from posture checks (AV, patch, encryption) for executive dashboards and exception reporting.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ise:posture`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_cisco-ise. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:posture. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:posture", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **check_pass** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by endpoint_mac** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliance_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where compliance_pct < 80` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (lowest-scoring endpoints), Histogram (score distribution), Gauge (fleet average score).
